# Lockfile Format

go2nix uses TOML lockfiles to pin module hashes and (optionally) the package
dependency graph. Two formats exist.

## v2 format (default)

Generated by `go2nix generate`. Used by DAG and dynamic modes.

```toml
# go2nix lockfile v2. Generated by go2nix. Do not edit.

[mod]
"github.com/foo/bar@v1.2.3" = "sha256-abc..."
"golang.org/x/sys@v0.20.0" = "sha256-def..."

[replace]
"github.com/foo/bar@v1.2.3" = "github.com/fork/bar"

[pkg."github.com/foo/bar@v1.2.3"]
"github.com/foo/bar" = ["golang.org/x/sys/unix"]
"github.com/foo/bar/internal" = []
```

### Sections

**`[mod]`** — Module hashes. Each key is a composite `"path@version"` string,
each value is a `sha256-...` SRI hash of the module's GOMODCACHE directory.

**`[replace]`** — Module replacements (from `go.mod` `replace` directives).
Maps the original composite key to the replacement module path. Only
remote replacements are recorded; local `replace` directives (filesystem
paths) are not included.

**`[pkg]`** — Package-level dependency graph, grouped by module. Each module
key maps to a table of `import_path = [dependency_imports...]`. Only
present when generating with the full lockfile (no `--minimal` flag).

### Composite keys

Module keys use `"path@version"` format (e.g., `"golang.org/x/sys@v0.20.0"`).
This keeps each module uniquely identified and avoids collisions across
versions.

### Minimal lockfile

`go2nix generate --minimal` omits the `[pkg]` section, producing only `[mod]`
and `[replace]`. This is sufficient for vendor mode and dynamic mode, which
don't need the package graph at eval time.

## Vendor format (v1)

Generated by `go2nix generate --gomod2nix`. Used by vendor mode.

```toml
# go2nix lockfile (gomod2nix format). Generated by go2nix. Do not edit.

[mod."github.com/foo/bar@v1.2.3"]
version = "v1.2.3"
hash = "sha256-abc..."
replaced = "github.com/fork/bar"
```

### Differences from v2

| Aspect | v2 | Vendor (v1) |
|--------|-----|-------------|
| Mod values | Plain hash string | Attrset with `version`, `hash`, `replaced` |
| `[pkg]` section | Yes (optional) | Never |
| `[replace]` section | Yes | No (folded into `replaced` field) |
| Used by | DAG, dynamic | Vendor mode |

The v1 format is derived from the original
[gomod2nix](https://github.com/nix-community/gomod2nix) lockfile format.
go2nix generates it via the `--gomod2nix` flag, which internally builds
the v2 lockfile and converts it.

## Monorepo support

When `go2nix generate` is given multiple directories, all modules are merged
into a single lockfile. The `[pkg]` section is grouped by module, so packages
from different `go.mod` files coexist without conflict.

## Staleness detection

| When | What | How |
|------|------|-----|
| Generation | MVS consistency | `go list -json -deps` resolves actual versions |
| Nix eval | Missing packages | Package not in lockfile triggers error |
| Build time | Lockfile consistency | `go2nix check --lockfile` validates against `go.mod` |
| Build time | Tidiness | `go mod graph` against GOMODCACHE from vendor tree |

Run `go2nix check <dir>` or `go2nix check --lockfile <path> <dir>` to verify
a lockfile without building.
